title: jasione crispa subsp. varduliensis
intro: Jasione crispa subsp. varduliensis is a subspecies of the Jasione crispa plant, a flowering perennial native to the Mediterranean region. It belongs to the bellflower family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and inflammation, due to its antispasmodic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Jasione crispa subsp. varduliensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
